target.title: feat: materialize shrinkwrapped runtime plugins
target.body:
# Human written summary:
The intent of this change is, as written by a human:
> all users want is to be able to use nix-openclaw with plugins the same way that they do with upstream openclaw, but declaratively. its that simple. we want to figure out how to implement that here in simplest possible way but no simpler; with a minimum of retarded shit and hacks

_The rest of this PR was written by GPT-5 Codex-unknown, running in the codex harness. Full environment + prompt history appear at the end._

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

# Changes
- Adds user and maintainer docs that compare regular OpenClaw plugin install commands with the nix-openclaw declarative equivalent for bundled, catalog, npm, ClawHub, npm-pack, git, local, and marketplace plugin sources.
- Adds RFC 2 for runtime plugin artifact materialization and updates RFC 1 so examples no longer describe now-supported WhatsApp as skipped.
- Extends the generated runtime plugin lock pipeline to admit package roots whose runtime deps are reproducible through bundled `node_modules` or package-local `npm-shrinkwrap.json` plus generated `npmDepsHash`.
- Adds ClawHub npm-pack artifact resolution at lock update time, with fixed tarball hash/integrity validation and fail-closed materialization checks.
- Adds narrow shrinkwrap normalization: when a shrinkwrap already selected a dependency package/version, the generator normalizes dependency specs to that selected version so offline npm replay follows the lock instead of trying to solve a range from the registry. It does not resolve latest, choose alternate versions, or trust unlocked registry state.
- Keeps the user API unchanged: supported catalog plugins are selected with `programs.openclaw.runtimePlugins = [ "id" ];`, never `npm:` or `clawhub:` source strings.
- Regenerates OpenClaw v2026.6.1 runtime plugin locks: 34 supported rows, 4 skipped rows. Seven supported roots are shrinkwrapped: `acpx`, `codex`, `copilot`, `matrix`, `memory-lancedb`, `tlon`, and `whatsapp`.
- Supports ClawHub-backed catalog rows in the current lock: `matrix` and `whatsapp` resolve through ClawHub npm-pack artifacts and build as immutable runtime plugin roots.

# Tests
- `node --check nix/scripts/update-openclaw-runtime-plugin-locks.mjs && node --check nix/scripts/check-openclaw-runtime-plugin-locks.mjs && node --check nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-plugin-install.mjs && node --check nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-plugin-prepare-npm.mjs && git diff --check` passed.
- `./nix/scripts/update-openclaw-runtime-plugin-locks.mjs --check` passed: 34 supported, 4 skipped.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#packages.aarch64-darwin.openclaw-runtime-plugin-acpx .#packages.aarch64-darwin.openclaw-runtime-plugin-codex .#packages.aarch64-darwin.openclaw-runtime-plugin-copilot .#packages.aarch64-darwin.openclaw-runtime-plugin-matrix .#packages.aarch64-darwin.openclaw-runtime-plugin-memory-lancedb .#packages.aarch64-darwin.openclaw-runtime-plugin-tlon .#packages.aarch64-darwin.openclaw-runtime-plugin-whatsapp` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-plugin-locks .#checks.aarch64-darwin.config-validity .#checks.aarch64-darwin.gateway-smoke .#checks.aarch64-darwin.default-instance` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.aarch64-darwin.ci` passed.
- Local ClawSweeper-style preflight passed after one P3 stale-doc finding was fixed in RFC 1.
- Earlier remote checks were green for the previous head; this head needs the new remote check run to finish after push.

# Risks
- The lock updater is heavier than before because it now resolves npm/ClawHub artifacts, computes npm dependency hashes, and runs materialization probes. That is intentional maintainer-time cost, not user activation cost.
- The generated package contract expands: `matrix`, `tlon`, and `whatsapp` join the supported runtime plugin outputs at this head, in addition to the earlier shrinkwrapped promotions.
- Shrinkwrap normalization is deliberately narrow. If a future package lacks a complete shrinkwrap entry, integrity, or resolved artifact for a runtime dependency, it still fails closed instead of solving from the registry.
- PR #100 overlaps conceptually because it also moves default gateway packaging toward npm shrinkwrap. This PR deliberately does not merge PR #100's gateway rewrite; it only carries the small `sourceInfo` compatibility strip for `gatewayNpmDepsHash` and `acpxNpmDepsHash` so the runtime-plugin updater remains compatible if #100 lands first.

# Follow-ups
- Weixin, Yuanbao, and WeCom are the remaining real packageability gaps in the current report: their published tarballs declare runtime dependencies but do not include `npm-shrinkwrap.json`, package lock, or bundled `node_modules`. Under this support contract, upstream should publish a shrinkwrap or bundle runtime dependencies before nix-openclaw promotes them.
- PixVerse has one supported row and one skipped duplicate catalog row; that is catalog hygiene, not a packageability blocker for the emitted `pixverse` id.
- Arbitrary user-supplied npm/git/local/marketplace runtime plugin sources remain out of scope for `runtimePlugins` until nix-openclaw has a locked source-record API with source hash and dependency hash.

# Prompt History

## Environment
Harness: codex
Model: GPT-5 Codex
Thinking level: max
Terminal: zsh on macOS
System: macOS 26.5

## Prompts
| ISO-8601 | Prompt |
| --- | --- |
| 2026-06-05T16:20:00+02:00 | `Goal: design the next architecture step for nix-openclaw support of OpenClaw's upstream runtime npm/ClawHub plugin model. Do not patch repos yet. Do not create worktrees.` |
| 2026-06-05T16:40:00+02:00 | `all users want is to be able to use nix-openclaw with plugins the same way that they do with upstream openclaw, but declaratively. its that simple. we want to figure out how to implement that here in simplest possible way but no simpler; with a minimum of retarded shit and hacks` |
| 2026-06-05T16:55:00+02:00 | `what about clawhub does that shrinkwrap? the poitn isn't security the point is pacakging. if they have shrinkwwrapping then we have immutable installs OOTB and its easy, if not, then fuck off. right? go back to overall goal for the session: how do we make this work for our original north star of users being able to install plugins same way as in upstream?` |
| 2026-06-05T17:05:00+02:00 | `sure thats fine. okay then i think we have everyhting, simple as possible user config, as few knobs as possible, we support everything that has shrinkwrapped, we have a config, etc, this should be clear?` |
| 2026-06-05T17:05:30+02:00 | `lets create a worktree for implementing this and PR it. lets use [$session-goal-writer](/nix/store/hcks99ivrjhg2pg09lc97cqhd2ixh1w1-source/skills/session-goal-writer/SKILL.md) , success should be: written RFC that passes adversarial review (step one)) written execplan that passes adversarial review (step two) and written implementation and PR with green CI that also passes clawsweeper rubrics (locally [$clawsweeper-preflight](/Users/josh/.codex/skills/clawsweeper-preflight/SKILL.md) ) and remotely. is that enough info to start implementation while i go cook dinner?` |
| 2026-06-05T20:37:00+02:00 | `the docs as they stand in your PR are either wrong or misleading. they say we don't support a bunch of plugins when the whole point of this change was that we DO? any shrink wrapped plugin will work? so this is really not good enough. the whole point is that we can install all plugins (that are shrink wrapped) and tell end users that we have basically the same support contract as upstream. so either the docs are wrong or the feature isn't implemented as we discussed? i also wanna see a full adversarial review against all our criteria in session goal to ensure docs are fully coherent and up to date` |
| 2026-06-05T20:45:00+02:00 | `btw if some of the core plugins aren't packageable i wanna know why. do we have to shrinkwrap them? or must we create a PR to those plugins? (don't do it yet) also be aware of the other thread working on shrinkwrapping entire nix openclaw, there's some overlap there, i think you should merge first but let's see. also be aware that you might be able to learn things from those changes and vice versa so make sure to read the PR` |
target.author: joshp123
